props.title: Justice on Clawbank
props.subtitle: Agents, companies, friction, and building through the AI timeline

cards.title: Agents need more than wallets
cards.body: Clawbank moves from giving an agent a bank account toward giving your agent a company: economic agency plus legal and operational agency.

cards.title: Friction as the roadmap
cards.body: The hard parts are not distractions. Crypto workflows, legal wrappers, private-key handling, deployment, security, and trust become the map.

cards.title: Engineering did not get easier
cards.body: Model power raises the abstraction level, but it also raises the cost of not understanding your system.

cards.title: The AI timeline as psychological pressure
cards.body: Constant demos can make builders feel their work expires tomorrow. The useful question becomes not just what can I build, but what should I build.

cards.title: Building as art, not just adoption
cards.body: Some launches matter because they were worth doing before the market validates them. Build for what should matter next.
